clean_body: | | | | 24-Hour Push | Peter, We know Mike wrote to you the other day to let you know about the dire financial situation we’re in. The Democrats outraised us by TWELVE MILLION DOLLARS in Q1, and if we want Q2 to be any different, we have to make April, the first month of the quarter, a huge success. So we sat down with Mike and we came up with a plan. If we can raise $100,000 in the next 24 HOURS Mike will be able to authorize a DOUBLE-MATCH on all contributions, effectively guaranteeing that we hit our goal. | Thank you, Team Lawler | \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Mike Lawler Subject: Starting Q2 Strong: To: Peter, I have some bad news to share with you today... Just remember – this $12,000,000 is ON TOP of the $45,000,000 they’ve already pledged to spend to defeat me... There’s no way to sugarcoat this. If I’m going to stand a chance at keeping my seat next November we have to pick up the pace on our fundraising. That all starts right now – our April End-of-Month Deadline is the FIRST deadline of Q2 and we have to start out strong if we want to make up for Q1. | | | | | | Many aspects of politics are confusing, convoluted, and complicated – but one thing that couldn’t be more simple is this: Whoever raises the most money, usually wins. I hate to say it, but it’s true. So if we don’t pick up the pace to combat the millions being spent against me, we could lose – and if I lose Republicans will almost certainly lose control of the House as well. We can’t let that happen, Peter. The fate of this country hangs in the balance. You’ve never let me down before, I hope I can count on you to stand with me now. Thank you, | | Congressman Mike Lawler NY-17 | | | | | | Mike Lawler is a Republican running for reelection in New York’s 17th Congressional District. He is passionate about standing with law enforcement and making our communities more safe, protecting Social Security and Medicare, fixing our crumbling roads and bridges, and restoring the SALT deduction to lower taxes and make New York more affordable. | | USE THE ADDRESS BELOW TO CONTRIBUTE BY CHECK LAWLER FOR CONGRESS PO BOX 87 SOUTH SALEM, NY 10590 GET YOUR MIKE LAWLER, NO CUOMO MERCH No New Yorker wants to call our Tappan Zee Bridge the “Governor Mario M. Cuomo Bridge”. Proudly send the message that you want the bridge restored as the Tappan Zee. | Are you interested in staying up-to-date on Mike Lawler's reelection battle? Don't miss out on the latest news and updates from his campaign! By texting "Lawler" to 85007, you can receive regular updates on Mike's campaign, upcoming events, and important news about the race. Text "Lawler" to 85007 today and join the fight for a brighter future for New York! Do you know someone who would be interested in supporting Mike Lawler’s reelection campaign for Congress? Don't keep it to yourself! Spread the word and remind your friends and family to join the fight for America’s future. Simply forward this message to five of your friends or family members who you think would be interested in staying up-to-date with Mike's campaign. FORWARD THIS EMAIL As we get closer to the 2024 election, Mike Lawler is more motivated than ever to continue our journey toward a better future for all New Yorkers. To stay up-to-date with our latest news and events, we encourage you to text "Lawler" to 85007 and follow us on social media, where we share updates, thoughts, and news about our progress. You can also opt others in by providing their email address and we will send them regular updates and important news about the campaign. | go.txting.io/7jAGfc Let's work together to ensure that Mike Lawler has the support he needs to make a real difference for New York! | | | | | \- =

[**Our end-of-month deadline is TOMORROW at MIDNIGHT, and right now we’re still not at our goal.**](http://links.right-gop.com/u/click?_t=2ba4f933732b46f5b6eae32741d1832c&_m=729e2e8be4f4440f8ecfb61ecc097b98&_e=L7CS7TkHy0hFtNYcb6TalokUk3IGC1n3ouh1C847lIKBc73aPnR4KBea37TLUfPP88sFM8_V6_JBCAD-pZQZn3pxudAODsL1q6XHl_THYFYLxy1TaRpa5GAiIwLIXREu5LfjvwIKr1Rtt6aa4WIYAOX_mr9-gPf3vB0xDQFs0hfd6P2SMGp3uKhGm7FF2CU-HcxwfhDhRJ6JHwDWI0tRoEvVu1HSkwU9caQmD4a1537u8ZoapqRwKOgraptBVn4RJf9HzIn6IYpBrGq6DCHUq5q3RDoukhE7Cm9Vobzm4-29wPhC7pls73tUKdiXYJubvUkozFsEbXyEgjH9UTZajOTAOlNFDg2C2LLBImsWq-GvsSuNyrsUs8CJyxuOR9MW_QYtgdGDdyNrlR4b_82DWA%3D%3D)

name: Cyrus Shick, Campaign Manager
subject: pushing for a bit more 📈
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Campbell for Mayor
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230429_em_fr_b1_CS_initial [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230429_em_fr_b1_CS_initial]
Hi Peter, it’s Cyrus, Heidi’s campaign manager.
Heidi always inspires me and the rest of the folks on Team Campbell to push a little bit further. When she’s not fighting for us in the state legislature, she’s out knocking on doors, attending meet-and-greet events, and giving this campaign everything she's got.
To win Nashville’s most competitive race of 2023, it's going to require all of us to push a little bit further.
Peter, our first End of Month Fundraising Deadline is tomorrow at MIDNIGHT and we're not on pace to meet the remaining $9,627 of our goal right now. Will you chip in whatever you can to push us over the top? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230429_em_fr_b1_CS_initial]
KEEP US PUSHING [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230429_em_fr_b1_CS_initial]
I admire lots of things about Heidi, but the thing I admire the most is her commitment to defending Nashville from nonsensical statewide policies.
Heidi is the only candidate in this race who has already been a mayor, and she’s running to put Nashvillians first and keep our community healthy, affordable, and safe.
Heidi is exactly the sort of leader Nashville needs and deserves, Peter.
Peter, tomorrow at MIDNIGHT marks a really important moment in our race – the first End of Month Fundraising Deadline for our campaign – and we need every bit of people-power behind us. We need another $9,627 to meet our End of Month Fundraising Goal. Can you help us reach it with a contribution today? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230429_em_fr_b1_CS_initial]
REACH OUR GOAL [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230429_em_fr_b1_CS_initial]
With gratitude,
Cyrus, Campaign Manager
Campbell for Mayor
Heidi Campbell is a Nashville native, Mom, former music industry executive and the first female mayor of the city of Oak Hill.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230429_em_fr_b1_CS_initial [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230429_em_fr_b1_CS_initial]
As mayor, Heidi established a track record for fiscal discipline, balancing the budget every year of her Mayorship, eliminating my own salary, and returning $2M to my city’s reserves.
Heidi was elected in November of 2020 to represent District 20 in the Tennessee State Senate. Since then, she’s been an outspoken advocate for science, the LGBTQ community, public education, local control, women's and kids' issues, Medicaid expansion, and the environment.
Heidi sponsored or co-sponsored over 100 pieces of legislation in her first year in the senate, including legislation to allow cities to offer paid family leave, increase funding for school counselors and reading interventionists, and make it easier to cast a vote.
Heidi is focused on sustainable infrastructure, economic development and job creation, fully funding our public schools and supporting educators, expanding access to affordable healthcare, investing in suicide prevention, making certain that vulnerable children in state custody are not falling through the cracks, and ensuring that every Tennessean - regardless of ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age or ability - can achieve the American Dream.
Election Day is Thursday, August 3, 2023.
Prefer to donate via mail? Address a check to:
Campbell for Mayor
P.O. Box 330302
Nashville, Tennessee 37203
SUPPORT HEIDI [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230429_em_fr_b1_CS_initial]
Paid for by Campbell for Mayor
Chip Forrester, Treasurer
Paid for by Campbell for Mayor, Chip Forrester, Treasurer
Campbell for Mayor
PO Box 330302
Nashville, TN 37203
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/3Q2nAB9rSEOu2DNcGzejHQ2?unsubscribedata=gCCI%2B0Gm4LDKKh98owxm4BYZzIFCUjUQZ9DoKsGzVDiUcFN2CE7h8I6d5akuVGmHOaPQALp4JwbP5hYCxFn3zBCLhXObkGFYNU%2BG170l60NUAkXjpaG9w0epmwBWndQvNam8nfvHuPqi1QyYIczT%2FZH2m4jckmgxetVyq4pjAaezyTWlzfvsVU1hX5jX5PG11k%2FNUg2GjON9C699%2BHpWay6xb%2FLDA2N8wq%2FlxPfBYSYk3Ok4rg18CRG1en6qctJ46Ab%2F6b2AIYWyAuYHe8LDGfm03arA2VpTRdAykm47e7iOYtkbNY1uJHd%2BaZHlMMNaQ2LEVfBZLjxp5YQ5mQsxTzB0F%2B8%2Bfbt8GmjvgHitb6ZD3J1u03oAYG%2F5apixoZtmEUQg4Bblwop%2Fb4xHz8W6sJ%2BjhsurAbTiy9sAkmUpCU1seGmE867Fyy9p5Dv5ptBO .

name: =?utf-8?Q?Rep.=20Jake=20LaTurner?=
subject: Biden is AWOL (2 min update)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kansans for LaTurner
body:
Pushing back on wasteful spending | |
---
| | | [ ](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=a4acd03cca&e=1050d051da)
---
| Friend,

It's Jake LaTurner here. House Republicans are delivering on the promises they made to the American people and it’s driving Democrats **CRAZY!**

I wanted to take a minute to give you an update on what we worked on this week in Washington when it comes to the debt ceiling and how Republicans passed legislation to push back on the extreme Green New Deal agenda and prioritize American manufacturers over Communist China.

Thanks to all your help, we are **FINALLY** getting America back on track. I am so grateful for your continued trust and support.

Yours in the fight,
Jake
---
|
---
| **JOE BIDEN IS AWOL ON THE DEBT LIMIT**
---
| [ ](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=e95c6c7c2d&e=1050d051da)
---
| [ ](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=6723917f05&e=1050d051da)
---
| Joe Biden recklessly spent our hard-earned tax dollars and maxed out America's credit card—spending over **$10 TRILLION** since taking office. Thanks to Joe's spending spree, Americans have faced record inflation and a crumbling economy.

Now the bill is due and President Biden refuses to come to the table and negotiate with Republicans to get Washington's fiscal house in order.

**Here's the bottom line: The President's lack of leadership has put the American economy in jeopardy.**

This week House Republicans stepped up and passed a plan to end Hakeem Jeffries and Joe Biden's out-of-control spending once and for all.

Democrats **HATE** that Republicans passed a conservative, pro-growth plan and are spreading lies about our package. I want to set the record straight.

**Here's what the Republican proposal****DOES****do: **
✅ Saves $4.8 trillion over 10 years
✅ Caps federal spending at 1%
✅ Blocks Biden's student loan bailout
✅ Ends Green New Deal tax cuts
✅ Defunds 87,000 IRS agents
✅ Unleashes American energy

**Here's what the Republican proposal****DOES NOT****do:**
❌ DOES NOT cut veterans' benefits
❌ DOES NOT cut Social Security
❌ DOES NOT cut Medicare
---
|
---
| **FIGHTING THE EXTREME GREEN NEW DEAL AGENDA**
---
| [ ](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=d50b512cad&e=1050d051da)
---
| **Joe Biden and AOC promised to destroy the American energy industry with their extreme Green New Deal agenda. House Republicans are the only ones preventing their socialist dreams from becoming a reality. **

This week I voted to **BLOCK** Biden's policy that puts Communist China over American workers and manufacturers. Democrats want to make it **EASIER** to get solar panels from China than from businesses in America.

That's unacceptable. Republicans promised that we would put **AMERICA FIRST** and** ** that's exactly what we are doing. [[Read more here]](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=82d823e382&e=1050d051da)
---
|
---
| **HOUSE REPUBLICAN LEGISLATIVE UPDATE**
---
| I am proud to have voted in favor of legislation on the House Floor that **REVERSES** Joe Biden's far-left policies and puts **AMERICA FIRST** :

✅ Defund Joe Biden’s IRS army
✅ Protect parents' rights
✅ Block gov't officials from colluding with Big Tech to censor speech
✅ Declassify all info relating to the COVID-19 lab leak
✅ Rein in Biden's reckless federal spending
✅ Strengthen penalties for violent crimes
✅ Protect women's sports
✅ Stop Democrats from forcing their woke climate agenda into retirement funds
✅ Create a committee to hold Communist China accountable
✅ Create a subcommittee on the weaponization of government
✅ Push back on Democrats' extreme pro-abortion agenda
✅ Unleash domestic energy production
✅ Officially end the COVID-19 national emergency

---
| [DONATE NOW TO DEFEND THE HOUSE GOP MAJORITY](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=43e51db010&e=1050d051da "DONATE NOW TO DEFEND THE HOUSE GOP MAJORITY")
---
| | Paid for by Kansans for LaTurner
---
|

|

name: Tim Burchett
subject: Fighting For What’s Right 💪

| | **Fighting For What’s Right 💪**
---
[](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=61da91c87bde430797c38abce022280c&_e=GposZv001p9WeIu-mloxXJsJqHocLS21DCrek8cr61ALbQS3P2C3A7g4nxFvyZPQ_nvHBfTNVL3ZHaRSLjHljD8ogJBvZCmc2BSEupUEcfKe-5Gdle_sCbI4QJR-9lx_L-HTNnRguqxZ2V8JR1VHthamoiKyUCWVrVOtkVNWZPMvBSPpk2eAl_f3gBK4OmC-tA0UY5L3om9cedGd_fwI9Usx7lTu5nNOeoBAZd9Z4oBGxXpDGVWEqFXOx7by2EnUFN5rWnrlYCDu8eXMBclQOHMQcLhv2eAmeWUwJIte9-txfPKeFJX3xcygY48mMHx5AnQPLhkSB6fhCQMlllyHdA%3D%3D)
---
| | Let’s just call this the hill I’m willing to die on. I don’t care who is in charge; I have never voted to raise the debt limit and likely never will. Why? Because America is nearly [$32 trillion](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=61da91c87bde430797c38abce022280c&_e=GposZv001p9WeIu-mloxXMJcFbXbeUTSCVSriPLKyoy9vaWc-nF-hGYzzV5aJu8BRHUGRMMIUyV27msaedFKCccJLTbuGDcNk7dAwmdgjj8FlZU8rtLdwdpftYSOtZJElszuCFGgyGpHQ6JuLO2KzqlMHnhYt5HGPDUIpswjkHqFH0-mk1LMW1oDXDG3IFSgjwpjeT3KQdQIULWsN7HIj2oQALQP60JVRAgW-UOXSYJBVuMGFG3v1T6ILyg3d0CHABFdOnL3LC0xv-KmwpxGJC8ftbOOUYw2-qtpYfR9xIeTBSnrc7kWscCW5Gyd5otx) in debt right now. That’s right, 32,000,000,000,000...a figure with a whopping 12 zeros. Knowing this, I voted _against_ the debt ceiling legislation that [narrowly passed in the House](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=61da91c87bde430797c38abce022280c&_e=GposZv001p9WeIu-mloxXOe0GhhnoQsWsdPwEasXeaxzKZfU8whrW7uI64Yu-lT5P57-zc16KVlfvq9uIGbTqv80VCENElX9osIECLZbwisb1YP5odAYXHDyfOVVsSqlPnSg_F7zd295XyjlYZr9_jzTYvHeYBjMpi_DJEYHPRSqbVc_FN09tSgNNVWxWcPkF0tu4epU28_Rwg2NTGc0v58MM2lVOX3l-vu-5V7QCnVT-Z0_mDMld3i6abCnNeM4krNBj5dH4j3B15-Qf_sY0tAk2tnPZCcAG4hvoI4ly07WSbIlz-UoFUqiYsD5tMVq) this week. Sure, I was one of just four Republicans who stood up for what’s right—but that’s fine with me. As East Tennessee’s elected representative, I aim to stay true to the shared values that got me here in the first place. I understand that those values aren't just fancy talk—they're the bedrock of our community. That's why I'm committed to standing up for what's right and fighting for the folks who put their faith in me. I’ve said it hundreds of times, and I’ll say it hundreds more—this reckless spending and never-ending taxation has got to stop. If we don't put an end to it, our children’s children’s children will be living with the consequences of our [foolish spending sprees](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=61da91c87bde430797c38abce022280c&_e=GposZv001p9WeIu-mloxXKi0Hh7D4Vr0BaY498pqnuVwwGkflm5-edzDkunvTh6U5L9f5zZT50a9C4AifU09bTUiTs0E0ic_-BsMPOj3gBIIPUI5moNPZVx-FKj5jHMjQEbI5ji1pq5e-L8l0-rQkmb1guyA2nKa9OREA5_sogSWwsBnnayV6_AA5pCC2tH133nBO1lJTIapKkZXyzFBNgYB_jAO1RykGQ2w4KfdtDc-ysNmEEdIApZDvOl3wuM8QkB0HU6SWDAOXZZ4F6BmD7pZ7LGcgdNRw8QKIZA2Jts%3D). But it’s not only the boldfaced spending I’m worried about. It also grinds my gears that there’s a severe [lack of transparency](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=61da91c87bde430797c38abce022280c&_e=GposZv001p9WeIu-mloxXOe0GhhnoQsWsdPwEasXeay2W-MiOvx2Z0yw5CZ3S9yCKrBfQU2gUBXxwwybG7pJBY9FqFHWiC9tQxRqW4L7aL1qK7XQ6UvTBYnmrtFuAoe3BKbHzURR1J2WEtmwZVl6vdgnxeL9wWYYJy9xVDMYpfzNbKhjhxqQkf8LxdMQ3HaZIjmmJoiBjQ4sGonOYt4bAxnofxphB843NVrcV6e2bQ2_rxRczfSu9pc3Qp0BYDfTXzF6r-QnKuHvYcANr3qhgORmCnt__r1snb_zUI3efWLNugJmL8lDbWndZ4QLjofC_xIg5ah2xHIgdW2oZOYE4KyUCBLlKAR1Uf9fEh_r6xA%3D) when it comes to how our tax dollars are being spent in Washington. I mean, this is your money we're talking about! You work hard for it, and you deserve to know where it's going. But let me tell you, I'm not seeing nearly enough accountability from those in charge. That’s why we must hold our elected officials' feet to the fire and demand better. Today, tomorrow, and always, you can count on me to stay true to the promises I made to each and every one of you. It often feels like my back’s against the wall, but I’ll keep fighting for fiscal responsibility until the very end.
---
| | |
---
| | **Smoky Mountains Magic** ✨
---
[](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=61da91c87bde430797c38abce022280c&_e=GposZv001p9WeIu-mloxXMPlj6LBrr0-NNvkHG5v4_qJXn_NwgQz-Yhc-wo8SqBK06QJJvP_an-9dCtae7ErDX6OTORsWFtJtfdU3g_k7096nOY245lRnjrOAPdN49Uwpr8W6M0qy27HvSLw0vRQ4VoYE-XSElJDlYlwy3ddi2RmZjCiEHAFnZ0n6e82Gzsi07U6UT6q_Y76Tk-IGflVETLpm_PhZaAahRmza57u22jvC0ip7GzQx8z_Y9gDB3f1qMOZwCyIP5ckI6UqdLXdxCabSOb_SXc0KP4UOIluZDA%3D)
---
# Photo Credit: National Park Service
---
| | It’s hard to believe our treasured Great Smoky Mountains National Park could get any better, but this spectacle may seal the deal.
The [synchronous firefly viewing event](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=61da91c87bde430797c38abce022280c&_e=GposZv001p9WeIu-mloxXOTFSRyKUnlw4GIRj8JY9IbG428pg8cU026wAVkyaTDNmFB8P2PLtT92-YRSGzBl2cHW_4pvCgVxmELbITarh0fvcsI0JAzUgKiCSE6_4iWqJozUPnBwzs8pwSyjSWvXat30x7r_GRPlI18ffyfCUeiXsv-vjpg9CpJC77atNh1YCA0dZjguoPJLNdNUaMShhgNsJvkwc8G-cc6lSO0hAuQMw6I1r3KdBCx9KSsVNZm66-K2-jS6gJ41BeBl2rxYKg%3D%3D) is right around the corner, and for [just two weeks](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=61da91c87bde430797c38abce022280c&_e=GposZv001p9WeIu-mloxXFQguTB7_fQeCijkfrCRbq0ObyFFTF_DfQcVXvdJkOUL9aIGR8QFFZYgiMpZfdFKpBVcjGxftUE6DwengdsIMMshQj_YKiPRTBHvsNXktGNGTE00p998VKx0bdZdG1d5IrqDLC8eWnrajN2_jhzNbwu_HfUbPWHkuGdhcvfll3b3mRvfS68ENS7utaHMclmFzBF9_BCToZmK5Q98QsIwI8U%3D), these fireflies will put on a beautiful display. In an effort to find their perfect mate, these insects light up the night in harmony, which makes for a truly dazzling experience.
Why do they all shine at once? According to the National Park Service, it’s so females know they’re responding to one of their kind. That’s because there are 19 different species of fireflies living in Great Smoky Mountains National Park.
Tennessee is blessed with some of the most breathtaking outdoor sights in the country, and I couldn’t be more proud to call this place home.

name: Rick Scott, me
subject: We’re missing your name

| | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. I'm running to win.** [](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953x8Bdi3DJx_tI_BkDTloM8ojNLW3Zfjzelbu2Y4K6WvKtgULVJFF-FTrpswhwq-vJwqiXCmTRy_fyg-nYDEPezGyI16OYPkIhulgTSx1dAa3QBNQ9y34FLr5ppijeVrYv0GnJ8EXJlrzGAJmMEB-Onidc6_rvfPMZPMgbInEFYsdSAEh54WQ9N2cLY4dsP0_d0WueD15gSoF6KgAAo1OUgEVpzRpLQmE-l4vrDzgIAxfBpKShPrfWurBpKSW7D_ynH4cr4BgF0CuU7_lwPmMXWtJ3WezLEWeTeSK9HD59D2ckHtPl-D3JzDoZP_HvfTn-etWZ_rbmGgDP6YDj4kF38s2FXjFpNUX7BzYXRYgmKWhyPWAoHpEaipD-WEqe9JCW665JAWnlqiOfy3bzaGSHls/3vr/gvl0a-nkSCi0HQ8FhEfyhA/h1/QPg5spuhtdakkkLUj0nC7quGtFy14wrJEwNtW5CZi3c) I have an incredible opportunity to win in California and become just the third Black woman ever to be elected to the U.S. Senate – where I can be the vote we need to abolish the filibuster, pass legislation to expand voting rights, fight for reproductive rights, combat climate change and build a progressive future for all of us – _but only if I have your help._

Initial polls show that a large majority of voters are still _undecided_ , meaning that this incredibly competitive race is still anyone's to win. But here's the issue: **I'm not only being outraised by the others in this race, MAGA extremists are also _attacking_ me and attempting to destroy my legacy.**

**That's why I'm counting on you to help post an incredibly strong End-of-Month showing so I can pull ahead, fight back and do what I came here to do: Win!**

[So please, before my End-of-Month fundraising goal, will you donate $10 or more to help me win and build the progressive future we deserve in the Senate?](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953x8Bdi3DJx_tI_BkDTloM8ojNLW3Zfjzelbu2Y4K6WvKtgULVJFF-FTrpswhwq-vJwqiXCmTRy_fyg-nYDEPezGyI16OYPkIhulgTSx1dAa3QBNQ9y34FLr5ppijeVrYv2vmSq2mRMYuBp40xaj66uzJmNS7KB00AiP92y3uIDXj7DXSdwlpo2xDsKtKm0VZSUJm0munVJrf95PUP6x9MkD92D-yMThfucBYXj0pzMXaIF67ny18mZnyF1sQOH13pgu-kE_B6vy4qruwftqsR0cKndrn99XvCLo6dPYM_TN-ZNls5Gy4vEoOL5UcbiXGf8Xp3UQ-9I3uKIlpvKaMLSBDyy02WwL6Ko6BVwxP1GO4X4q0_nzX-8n_nfmR3xh4EbT9Ic5htweX465oF1qoFPe/3vr/gvl0a-nkSCi0HQ8FhEfyhA/h2/IukS0CGdyeKCS0orycQUAI0qD99rL2VzCqrzk0rQg-g) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately and split between Barbara Lee and The Collective PAC.

clean_body: | | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. If you'd like to unsubscribe | | Hi Peter, right now, I'm running a campaign in what experts have said may be the most competitive and expensive Senate primary in history – and I'm falling short of my $100,000 end-of-month goal with only 2 days left to make up the difference. The road to victory may be tough, but make no mistake, I'm no stranger to a tough fight. I'm running to win. I have an incredible opportunity to win in California and become just the third Black woman ever to be elected to the U.S. Senate – where I can be the vote we need to abolish the filibuster, pass legislation to expand voting rights, fight for reproductive rights, combat climate change and build a progressive future for all of us – but only if I have your help. Initial polls show that a large majority of voters are still undecided , meaning that this incredibly competitive race is still anyone's to win. But here's the issue: I'm not only being outraised by the others in this race, MAGA extremists are also attacking me and attempting to destroy my legacy. That's why I'm counting on you to help post an incredibly strong End-of-Month showing so I can pull ahead, fight back and do what I came here to do: Win! | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately and split between Barbara Lee and The Collective PAC. | | | | | | Thank you for supporting my campaign and helping me make history. subject: We need 128 donations to this email
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail
body:
Yes, this one.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=125%2E791427%2E9OVZIs)
Friends -
As you read this, our team is keeping a close eye on where our fundraising stands ahead of tomorrow's end-of-month deadline, and we're still a bit behind where we need to be if we're going to reach our goals.
After Ilhan's email this morning, we crunched the numbers and figure that we need **128 donations to _this_ email** if we're going to keep pace.
**[Can you make a $3 contribution to stand with Ilhan and our people-powered campaign today? We're tracking donations at this link.](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-apr-29-23.HIYtVG&refcode=em230429-2-t2&t=2&akid=125%2E791427%2E9OVZIs)**
🔴👀 Here's a _**live look_** at where we're at right now:
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-apr-29-23.HIYtVG&refcode=em230429-2-t2&t=3&akid=125%2E791427%2E9OVZIs)
No matter when you open this email — whether the thermometer is close to full or just getting heated up — every single donation on it represents a fellow member of our movement chipping in what they can.
Now, they're counting on you to do the same. So please:
**Can you use this link to contribute $3 or anything you can to stand with Ilhan today?**
**[<https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/128-donations>** Like we've said before, the goals we set aren't arbitrary. They're what we need to hit to have the resources required to fight back and win. Thanks in advance, Team Ilhan **[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-apr-29-23.HIYtVG&refcode=em230429-2-t2&t=5&akid=125%2E791427%2E9OVZIs)**
PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can **[unsubscribe here](https://act.ilhanomar.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?source=em-footer&t=6&akid=125%2E791427%2E9OVZIs)**. You can also **[sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=7&akid=125%2E791427%2E9OVZIs)**. Submit this form to [**update your contact information**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/update-contact-info/source=em-footer?t=8&akid=125%2E791427%2E9OVZIs) Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **[making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/io-em-footer/?refcode=em-footer&t=9&refcode2=125_791427_9OVZIs&akid=125%2E791427%2E9OVZIs)**. Ilhan for Congress
PO Box 33079
Washington D.C., 20033

_*YOU ARE ONE OF 1,500 HAND-PICKED DEMOCRATS SELECTED BECAUSE OF YOUR COMMITMENT TO THE DEMOCRATIC PARTY. PLEASE, ANSWER HONESTLY BEFORE MIDNIGHT!*_
Team, as the first woman Vice President, Kamala Harris has broken one of the highest glass ceilings in our country.

We think Harris will go down in history as one of the most important figures in American history.

_But Republicans are smearing her DAILY in hopes of sabotaging Democrats’ chances of retaining the White House in 2024._

Friend,

Even though House Republicans have spent the first months of this year aligning themselves with MAGA extremism and unpopular policy ideas, their fundraising has not suffered.

_Axios_ just reported that in the districts likely to decide control of Congress in 2024, **Republicans are raising an average of nearly $300,000 more than Democrats.**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62534535/403759342/219556864?refcode=EM_FR_230429EOM2_EOM_HQ_FULL_7_G_N_P1&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Njg0NjIxNy1kMmU2LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=73XH7K86d6X1t9VRcVos35vO6jgwJOYhlA-ieQtZPCo=)
It's clear that donor enthusiasm among Republicans is high — and that they will have the resources early.

Make no mistake: if we fail to hit our goals in these early months of the 2024 cycle, the GOP will be in a position to overwhelm us with attacks in VA-10 before we're in a position to respond effectively.

name: Jon Tester
subject: I’m proud of this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Susan Wild for Congress
body:
Every spring, I spend a lot of weeks working the stubble, getting equipment ready for planting, and out in the field seeding.

| Can I explain something, Peter?

I grew up in a twenty-four-by-thirty-six foot house. I lost three fingers on my left hand in a meat grinding accident when I was a kid. I am and always will be a farmer, like my folks and like my grandparents.

In fact, I’m very proud to be the only U.S. Senator who manages a full-time job outside the Senate —**as a farmer.**

Every spring, I spend a lot of weeks working the stubble, getting equipment ready for planting, and out in the field seeding. Come summer, I am out there harvesting. I maintain my own heavy equipment and deliver my grain in a truck that’s a bit bigger than what any of my colleagues drive.

I buy grease by the case and seeds by the ton. I butcher my own beef. My wife, Sharla, and I work the farm hand-in-hand.

The task of scheduling my work in the Senate around the inflexible timing of farm responsibilities drives my staff crazy — but they know my farm work is non-negotiable. It keeps me, quite literally, grounded.

**And rather than spend my days hobnobbing with Republican special interests, I rely on hard-working folks like you to power everything we do.

[_That’s why I’m personally asking you to join my reelection team and split $10 between Susan Wild and my campaign today to help us block Mitch McConnell from taking back the Senate. Anything you can give makes a difference._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62535275/403759587/-37640284?refcode=BLU-SWC-20230429-2-EM&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODczMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZTk3YTJkMC1kMmU2LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rx2T_deNyyUTlSwCvwMkVAtfVu3DHFoEi4KfGiuFi-U=)**

name: John Fetterman
subject: I have a question
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
Would you do it?
#
I have a question for you, Derek.
If you knew that chipping in a $5 donation *right now* could help us protect our Democratic Senate majority + achieve wins for working families nationwide...**would you do it?**
What if I told you that if you donated $5 right now, you would directly help us stand up to the hundred$ of million$ that the GOP will spend in battleground states like PA in 2024 + beyond?
This movement has *always* been powered by real people like you — not dirty corporate PACs or shady mega-donors. And now, with our next end-of-month deadline around the corner, I’m asking for your support:
**[If you’re ready to make a real difference, I need you to take action *right now*. Will you rush a donation of $5 or more to help support our work in the Senate + defend vulnerable Democratic seats in 2024?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20230429-1?refcode=em20230429-1-8351&refcode2=8351_434096_UJaf6i&akid=8351%2E434096%2EUJaf6i)**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20230429-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230429-8351&refcode2=8351_434096_UJaf6i&akid=8351%2E434096%2EUJaf6i)**
From serving Braddock as mayor to serving all of Pennsylvania as Lieutenant Governor, I’ve always been committed to the people of PA. Always have been, always will be.
And over the last two years, we built one of the most powerful grassroots movements in the country together. We’re by and for the people, and we’re stronger than the GOP’s dark money groups ever could be. 💪💪💪
**I’m so proud of what we’ve accomplished together, Derek. But now we need to ensure we’re prepared for the fights ahead.**
**[Will you make your first $5 donation to support our grassroots-funded work *right now*?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20230429-1?refcode=em20230429-1-8351&refcode2=8351_434096_UJaf6i&akid=8351%2E434096%2EUJaf6i)**
Thank you so much for all that you’ve done. I hope being part of this movement has made you proud.
❤️ John

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-emails?refcode=em20230429-8351-footer&t=1&refcode2=8351_434096_UJaf6i&akid=8351%2E434096%2EUJaf6i)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/JohnFettermanPA/) | [](https://twitter.com/johnfetterman) | [](https://www.instagram.com/johnfetterman/)
Email us: info@johnfetterman.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Fetterman for PA, PO Box 6061, Pittsburgh PA 15211.

Peter, In just a moment, we’re going to ask if you can spare $1 to help fund our efforts to elect more women and young people into Congress. [_**If you want, you can cut to the chase by donating your four quarters right here,**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62540761/403761921/-253429511?amount=1&refcode=AU_HTP_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20230429_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDI1NDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDIyZDZhODctZDhlNi1lZDExLThlOGItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=xjL1UQjreRcoRTbLWz36QrnePmZlzx6XnXIG5hUG2Is=) but here’s why it’s so important. Our end-of month goal expires tomorrow at midnight, and right now, we’re not on track to hit that goal. We need your help to catch up. $1 may not seem like a lot to you — especially compared to the billionaire mega donors and shady super PACs Republicans have in their corner — but we have the power of the grassroots on our side. $1 from every single person reading this email will add up quickly, and give us the kind of momentum we need to elect more women and young people and take back the House majority in 2024. We already know that the more our government looks like us, the more it works for us. We need more women in positions of leadership in order to make sure our voices are heard, and that we have a seat at the table. Otherwise, we’ll never make progress on codifying abortion access, paid family leave, closing the gender pay gap, and more. [_**Peter, if we told you the dollar sitting in your wallet could get us one step closer to making those priorities a reality, would you chip in today?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62540762/403761922/-253429511?amount=1&refcode=AU_HTP_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20230429_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDI1NDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDIyZDZhODctZDhlNi1lZDExLThlOGItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=xjL1UQjreRcoRTbLWz36QrnePmZlzx6XnXIG5hUG2Is=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $1 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62540763/403761923/-253429511?express_lane=true&amount=1&refcode=AU_HTP_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20230429_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDI1NDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDIyZDZhODctZDhlNi1lZDExLThlOGItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=xjL1UQjreRcoRTbLWz36QrnePmZlzx6XnXIG5hUG2Is=)

clean_body: | = Peter, In just a moment, we’re going to ask if you can spare $1 to help fund our efforts to elect more women and young people into Congress. = but here’s why it’s so important. Our end-of month goal expires tomorrow at midnight, and right now, we’re not on track to hit that goal. We need your help to catch up. $1 may not seem like a lot to you — especially compared to the billionaire mega donors and shady super PACs Republicans have in their corner — but we have the power of the grassroots on our side. $1 from every single person reading this email will add up quickly, and give us the kind of momentum we need to elect more women and young people and take back the House majority in 2024. We already know that the more our government looks like us, the more it works for us. We need more women in positions of leadership in order to make sure our voices are heard, and that we have a seat at the table. Otherwise, we’ll never make progress on codifying abortion access, paid family leave, closing the gender pay gap, and more. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thanks for your support, HER Time HER Time PAC 2120 University Ave Berkeley, CA 94704 United States | mailto:team@her-time.com | Paid for by the HER Time PAC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

body:
will you help us cross the April finish line?
Team, tomorrow is the last day of April, so I’d like to sincerely thank each and every one of you for pitching in to our end-of-month fundraiser. This team gives me hope for a more progressive future.
But, we are still running behind where we need to be. This might seem like a small amount in politics, but trust me, it makes all the difference. That’s more resources we have in the field, more communications efforts we can run, and more events for us to connect with voters.
These last few hundred bucks matter. So I’m asking, friends: Will you chip in and get us over the finish line?
You can donate at my personal link: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-April2023-FR-April?refcode=EricPersonal](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62540904/403761959/-1813554275?refcode=E21317-20230429-LastMinute-SL-DeadlineEOM-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMTMxMzBhOC1kOGU2LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hx5lylq-zbCTatzir8R7vZkkRoapcvZkQXQr6SADqvI=)

body:
Peter Willis,

Bernie Sanders is making waves as Chairman
of the HELP Committee, striking a bipartisan deal to lower prescription
drug prices that has Big Pharma and their lobbyists furious.

The markup for this bill begins on Tuesday, and Bernie is calling three
powerful pharma executives to testify next month and answer for their
outrageous price-gouging that contributes to the deaths of 68,000
Americans each year because they can't afford healthcare.

Big Pharma has triple the lobbyists per member of Congress, and Bernie's
plan to make them charge lower prices is their top target. [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8634?source=EM_NBI_OR_20230429_FR_EOMApril_0_6MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_EOM&t=1009&akid=4232%2E5885482%2EttTkxJ ]Will you

---
|
## Legislative Update for Week Ending April 28, 2023
---
|
## Pro-Life - LB626
---
| On Thursday, the inability to have 33 votes for cloture for the Heartbeat Bill was a gut punch for Republicans across Nebraska. Emotions of anger, crying, and shock went through the vast majority of Republicans.

Despite the loss, debate on the bill was inspiring and genuine. Many of our Republican legislators gave very personal and vulnerable stories of why they are pro-life. From those watching in the balcony, tears of empathy and the human connection to these stories was very palpable. This seemed like the historic moment to move pro-life legislation forward as the emotion of ‘why life is so precious’ was clearly and precisely orated. Unfortunately, the genuine speeches from the heart didn’t outweigh the politics of the few. The cloture vote failed by one vote and the bill did not advance.

The number of questions we received have been extensive. Both Chairman Underwood and myself as Political Director, have spent the last 24-36 hours on the phone with numerous senators and leaders in the Republican Party. From the senators, unicameral staff, to other elected officials, we have not stopped calling and texting to advance the values of our party forward. We want to provide clarity of what happened, where we BELIEVE this issue is going, and the vision we believe in for the party. For the brevity oriented, this is the ask. Call the Senators and ask for a Pro-Life amendment to a bill. Let them know swift action is needed! The NEGOP will not take this set back rolling over. We continue the article in Pro-LIfe Part II for those asking for more information. Readership that is short on time can skip to “Lincoln Municipality Election - Tuesday, May 2nd” for more brief summaries of the week’s activity.
---
|
## Pro-Life Part II (More Detail To Answer Questions)
---
| The timeline of this bill is important to understand. During the bill drafting process and at the beginning of the session, the senators had discussions on a life bill that could be passed. Senator Albrecht is on PUBLIC record in the Rotunda of wanting tougher legislation than the Heartbeat bill, but fully recognized the importance of passing “A” bill. She gave an interview to the media where she cited that LB626 was the bill that could be achieved and had the votes to pass. 30 senators co-sponsored this bill and BELIEVED that LB626 would pass. Senator Riepe signed his name as an original sponsor of LB626. Ultimately, his present/not voting position defeated the cloture vote and the bill. It is extremely uncommon for a sponsor of a bill to take actions against a bill they have signed in support. It should be also noted that Democrat Senator Mike McDonnell voted FOR Cloture, meaning LB626 had what appeared to be 33 votes to end the filibuster. Thank you Senator McDonnell. Senator Riepe’s amendment shifted the permitted abortion timing UP to 12-weeks from 6-weeks. Subsequently after the cloture vote failed, the 12-week amendment was never "considered" for voting AND the bill stalled. We must remember that LB626 had gone through stages of committee advancement to a General File vote where amendments could have been debated and voted on. The timing of this amendment was extremely controversial. Time existed for assembling, at the very least the 32 Republican Senators, to provide them all a chance to bring an objection BEFORE this bill started navigating the process.

Coming into 2023 the numbers needed to pass Pro-Life legislation were significantly better than 2022. The party and voters began to move more towards candidates that believed in advancing Republican Platform Values and away from Republican candidates who did not really possess our values. This is a vision away from the "politicians" that were there for power and not values. Platform and Constitutional Republicans were hopeful and supportive of the language in LB626 when unveiled. This was one of our first signs that the Conservative nature of our new body was solid. They appeared to have the votes to move 6 weeks (a very strong standard) forward. The bill would not have originally been presented ‘as is’ if the votes were not there behind the scenes to pass. As time passed, the bill remained on solid footing. However, the amendment for 12-weeks being presented in the first round was a concern and came out of left field to many Senators. Assurances were still made on the first round vote; the bill advanced. Between the first and second rounds it became clear the objection had grown. A Senator that demands to see the amendments of others was clearly not forthcoming with his own amendment. Furthermore, the rumors were growing that many in the medical community were putting significant pressure on several Senators including Senator Riepe. However, confidence was expressed by many Senators up until the vote that the cloture vote of 33 would pass the second round. The shock when this didn’t happen was apparent by statements made by many.

This leads us to ‘what happens next?’. There has been a discussion on “Reconsideration”. The most common form of “Reconsideration” happens after a vote on a BILL (not cloture). Reconsideration has to be initiated by either the Speaker, a member of the “Winning” side of the vote, or one who did not vote. In this case a Democrat, excluding Senator McDonell, or Senator Riepe. A “Reconsideration” motion of a CLOTURE VOTE is extremely rare (but has happened) per Speaker Arch. This reconsideration motion of a cloture vote can be made and the President (Lieutenant Governor Joe Kelly) must rule on the motion. The ruling of the chair has not advanced these motions in the past and the motion of reconsideration (due to a cloture vote) is very unlikely to occur. Again, this rare motion is possible but unlikely due the votes for 6 weeks not being there. Regardless, Senator Riepe reiterated he would not seek reconsideration on Friday. Speaker Arch has made it clear he will not reconsider LB626 for floor debate because of the precedence he set early on. This leads us to looking at an amendment to an existing bill. More on this later...

For context of the new leadership at the NEGOP, we know many bills in the past have amazingly come just one vote short throughout the political landscape (this is not specific to Nebraska). Oftentimes, when a bill fails by ONE vote there are OTHERS who may have objected to the bill or amendment, but are given "political cover" to vote in the affirmative. The term political cover is a reference to the importance of being ELECTED versus representation of campaign values. Politicians from across the country and in both parties, have a demonstrated history of desiring power and money that comes with elections over advancing the values of their voters. Many elected officials are consulted to seek electability before accountability by not showing their hand with a vote on the record. The political parties have often moved forward politicians, not platform value representatives, for power across the nation. This has produced laws, charters, and policies that have given ground to the extreme left time and time again. Make no mistake, with our Nebraska Nice, legislation and policy are NOT staying within a moderate middle. The pendulum has been swinging bit by bit to the extreme left for decades. Why does the influence on an elected official change from the year of campaigning, to less than a few months in the elected position? Furthermore, it creates problems with the voters to understand where a senator actually stands. As new party leadership, we want to break this philosophy. Our country and state needs strong leadership that is defined by solid values. We will seek votes on the record whenever possible, even if it leads to disappointment like Thursday. Republicans need to know where our elected officials stand when it matters most; especially on the issues of Life, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness. We can protect Nebraska from the far left with leaders that have convictions on values. Policy improvement over power is critical in 2023. Values over personalities.

We are calling for the Pro-life issue to see the floor again in 2023. While LB626 may have limited options to find the floor, we know the pro-life issue has the ability to see the floor through an amendment on another piece of legislation. It is time to see if all our Senators are truly onboard with a pro-life bill (even if it is a different standard than 6 weeks) and can deliver a stronger standard than the current 20 weeks as a collective body. We know this takes grit, a no give up attitude, and extra work.

As a disclaimer, we TRULY believe the overwhelming majority of our senators ARE PRO-LIFE. We believe it is critical to have a vote on pro-life occur this session even if it's not 6 weeks. We understand a different standard would likely have to be presented to pass. For the sake of the voting public, we need to see a vote on the record...even if that bill is a new standard. We know the standard will not likely appease the hurt or satisfy the hopes of the citizens/governing body who voted to affirm the Heartbeat bill standard of roughly 6 weeks. However, we need to chart a new course as a party and seek a vote on the record on a significant piece of legislation so our voters can make an informed decision in 2024 on the issue.
---
|
---
|
## CALL YOUR SENATOR AND INSIST ON A PRO LIFE AMENDMENT!!!
---
| Finally, there is yet one more issue where we will point to...the need for new Rules. The NEGOP Legislative Plan adopted last August called to “Reduce the cloture vote requirement so that 17 Senators cannot block legislation.” Specifically, the leadership of the NEGOP suggests the level should match the number (3/5th's or 30) to override a Governor Veto. What is good to override the Governor is good to override a filibuster. This would preserve the filibuster capabilities, but bring the rule in line to move legislation forward that is strong enough to override the singular wishes of a Governor and the demands of a radical minority seeking to halt progress. We will bring this discussion forth again for the 2024 session.
---
|
---
|
## Lincoln Municipality Election - Tuesday, May 2nd
---
| Have you called your mom in Lincoln to vote? Has your uncle in Lincoln returned his absentee ballot to the Election Commissioner? Have you called everyone you know in Lincoln to vote on May 2nd?

We can’t emphasize voting enough. Lincoln is in dire need of leadership change. Law and order, taxes, and infrastructure proceeds in the wrong direction each and every day. Do everything in your power to make sure everyone you know in Lincoln with commonsense shows up to the polls on Tuesday or has returned their ballot. The extra legwork will MAKE A BIG DIFFERENCE!!!
---
|
---
|
## Voter ID
---
| Voter ID language was unexpectedly delayed. We are now anticipating the presentation of these materials next week. The holdup is on the mail-in-voting language. There are four different options being debated by Republican leaders. Each option presents different challenges. From complying with the literal language of the amendment to being in observance of court rulings on the Constitutionality of a provision are the challenges being wrestled with. The quandary is that the language of the amendment doesn’t make it friendly to mail-in voting methods. Court cases on a national level for access to voting are being exhausted in legal research to insure a law passes constitutional muster. There is not a lot of middle ground between Constitutional cases and Nebraska’s adopted language.

We will be quickly digesting the language after the release. Although Nebraska has traditionally taken a path of following the lead of other states, our Constitutional Amendment language is a new national standard for Voter ID. This is an opportunity for the state to show ingenuity and leadership on a critical issue the public has voted for by initiative (65.45% in favor). The language of the bill will demonstrate how tough of a path senators are willing to take with an observance of Constitutional cases that currently exist. Regardless of what is passed (strong or weak), it is likely to be challenged in court by the left desiring less election integrity.
---
|
---
|
## Video Footage Advances
---
| We are pleased to report one of our planks to improve Government transparency, LB254, passed the second round. Senator Brewer’s bill to create a video archive of legislative debate was successful. We will continue to push for transparency to aid the public in evaluating their government.
---
|
## Agriculture
---
| It is time for a farm bill discussion again. The usual early talking points around the issue are occurring. SNAP programs, environmental provisions, and insurance coverages are standard operating procedures. However, the topic that stood out to us and really moves the needle is the protection amounts. These insurance amounts have not kept up with the cost of production. Discussion is occuring to significantly raise these protection points so that farmers have stronger risk protection that more closely represents a 2023 and beyond production price point. If SIGNIFICANT safety net amounts are approved, Nebraska could significantly benefit as the discussion of continued drought continues. Stay tuned as this legislation evolves through the year.
---
|
## Healthcare Shortages
---
| Senator Riepe has legislation ready to go to add $6 million in spending to an HHS bill to help hospitals address nursing shortages. A report was issued that Nebraska is on pace to be over 5,400 nurses short. The funds are designed to be used to address the staffing shortage.
---
|
---
|
## Taxes and Spending
---
| The budget committee voted out a budget that increased spending by 2.3% over the next two years. This was higher than the 1.5% the Governor was seeking. Medical reimbursement amounts and University adjustments are driving the increases. These additions in spending will raise Nebraska spending to just over $5.3 billion a year.

Meanwhile, the economic forecasting board lowered revenue projections by $80 million to $6.36 billion. These numbers matter. The efforts to resolve a fiscal footnote of $900 million to continue to make tax relief work a reality continues.
---
|
## A Disturbing Sign of the Times
---
| Sometimes the state of affairs demonstrates the condition of the culture. Democrat Senator Megan Hunt had a complaint filed against her with the NADC. Senator Hunt received speeches of defense from both sides of the aisle defending her actions. Many Senators found the complaint absurd.

Previously this year, Pastor Arin Hess also had a complaint of “Lobbying” filed against him with the NADC by a leftist organization. Pastor Hess calls himself a “Lobbyist for Jesus”. We noted that Senator Blood disagreed with the complaint dismissal of Pastor Hess. Furthermore, the PUBLIC statements of defense for a man who ministers to members of the Capitol were not there like they were for Senator Hunt. Both cases were being covered by the media. The difference of public treatment regarding two different public cases of NADC complaints signaled to us the sign of the times we live in.

We will continue to fight for religious freedom and liberty at the NEGOP. We thank you for focusing on the spiritual needs of the Capitol, Pastor Hess. Free exercise on....

Until Next Week....Keep praying for the unborn babies.
---
|
---
| Todd Watson
Political Director
NEGOP
---

clean_body: A momentum of historical legislative accomplishments! | | | | | | | Peter, | Legislative Update for Week Ending April 28, 2023 Pro-Life - LB626 Pro-Life Part II (More Detail To Answer Questions) CALL YOUR SENATOR AND INSIST ON A PRO LIFE AMENDMENT!!! Lincoln Municipality Election - Tuesday, May 2nd Voter ID Video Footage Advances Agriculture Healthcare Shortages Taxes and Spending A Disturbing Sign of the Times | | Legislative Update for Week Ending April 28, 2023 | Pro-Life - LB626 | On Thursday, the inability to have 33 votes for cloture for the Heartbeat Bill was a gut punch for Republicans across Nebraska. Emotions of anger, crying, and shock went through the vast majority of Republicans. Despite the loss, debate on the bill was inspiring and genuine. Many of our Republican legislators gave very personal and vulnerable stories of why they are pro-life. From those watching in the balcony, tears of empathy and the human connection to these stories was very palpable. This seemed like the historic moment to move pro-life legislation forward as the emotion of ‘why life is so precious’ was clearly and precisely orated. Unfortunately, the genuine speeches from the heart didn’t outweigh the politics of the few. The cloture vote failed by one vote and the bill did not advance. The number of questions we received have been extensive. Both Chairman Underwood and myself as Political Director, have spent the last 24-36 hours on the phone with numerous senators and leaders in the Republican Party. From the senators, unicameral staff, to other elected officials, we have not stopped calling and texting to advance the values of our party forward. We want to provide clarity of what happened, where we BELIEVE this issue is going, and the vision we believe in for the party. For the brevity oriented, this is the ask. Call the Senators and ask for a Pro-Life amendment to a bill. Let them know swift action is needed! The NEGOP will not take this set back rolling over. We continue the article in Pro-LIfe Part II for those asking for more information. Readership that is short on time can skip to “Lincoln Municipality Election - Tuesday, May 2nd” for more brief summaries of the week’s activity. | Pro-Life Part II (More Detail To Answer Questions) | The timeline of this bill is important to understand. During the bill drafting process and at the beginning of the session, the senators had discussions on a life bill that could be passed. Senator Albrecht is on PUBLIC record in the Rotunda of wanting tougher legislation than the Heartbeat bill, but fully recognized the importance of passing “A” bill. She gave an interview to the media where she cited that LB626 was the bill that could be achieved and had the votes to pass. 30 senators co-sponsored this bill and BELIEVED that LB626 would pass. Senator Riepe signed his name as an original sponsor of LB626. Ultimately, his present/not voting position defeated the cloture vote and the bill. It is extremely uncommon for a sponsor of a bill to take actions against a bill they have signed in support. It should be also noted that Democrat Senator Mike McDonnell voted FOR Cloture, meaning LB626 had what appeared to be 33 votes to end the filibuster. Thank you Senator McDonnell. Senator Riepe’s amendment shifted the permitted abortion timing UP to 12-weeks from 6-weeks. Subsequently after the cloture vote failed, the 12-week amendment was never "considered" for voting AND the bill stalled. We must remember that LB626 had gone through stages of committee advancement to a General File vote where amendments could have been debated and voted on. The timing of this amendment was extremely controversial. Time existed for assembling, at the very least the 32 Republican Senators, to provide them all a chance to bring an objection BEFORE this bill started navigating the process. Coming into 2023 the numbers needed to pass Pro-Life legislation were significantly better than 2022. The party and voters began to move more towards candidates that believed in advancing Republican Platform Values and away from Republican candidates who did not really possess our values. This is a vision away from the "politicians" that were there for power and not values. Platform and Constitutional Republicans were hopeful and supportive of the language in LB626 when unveiled. This was one of our first signs that the Conservative nature of our new body was solid. They appeared to have the votes to move 6 weeks (a very strong standard) forward. The bill would not have originally been presented ‘as is’ if the votes were not there behind the scenes to pass. As time passed, the bill remained on solid footing. However, the amendment for 12-weeks being presented in the first round was a concern and came out of left field to many Senators. Assurances were still made on the first round vote; the bill advanced. Between the first and second rounds it became clear the objection had grown. A Senator that demands to see the amendments of others was clearly not forthcoming with his own amendment. Furthermore, the rumors were growing that many in the medical community were putting significant pressure on several Senators including Senator Riepe. However, confidence was expressed by many Senators up until the vote that the cloture vote of 33 would pass the second round. The shock when this didn’t happen was apparent by statements made by many. This leads us to ‘what happens next?’. There has been a discussion on “Reconsideration”. The most common form of “Reconsideration” happens after a vote on a BILL (not cloture). Reconsideration has to be initiated by either the Speaker, a member of the “Winning” side of the vote, or one who did not vote. In this case a Democrat, excluding Senator McDonell, or Senator Riepe. A “Reconsideration” motion of a CLOTURE VOTE is extremely rare (but has happened) per Speaker Arch. This reconsideration motion of a cloture vote can be made and the President (Lieutenant Governor Joe Kelly) must rule on the motion. The ruling of the chair has not advanced these motions in the past and the motion of reconsideration (due to a cloture vote) is very unlikely to occur. Again, this rare motion is possible but unlikely due the votes for 6 weeks not being there. Regardless, Senator Riepe reiterated he would not seek reconsideration on Friday. Speaker Arch has made it clear he will not reconsider LB626 for floor debate because of the precedence he set early on. This leads us to looking at an amendment to an existing bill. More on this later... For context of the new leadership at the NEGOP, we know many bills in the past have amazingly come just one vote short throughout the political landscape (this is not specific to Nebraska). Oftentimes, when a bill fails by ONE vote there are OTHERS who may have objected to the bill or amendment, but are given "political cover" to vote in the affirmative. The term political cover is a reference to the importance of being ELECTED versus representation of campaign values. Politicians from across the country and in both parties, have a demonstrated history of desiring power and money that comes with elections over advancing the values of their voters. Many elected officials are consulted to seek electability before accountability by not showing their hand with a vote on the record. The political parties have often moved forward politicians, not platform value representatives, for power across the nation. This has produced laws, charters, and policies that have given ground to the extreme left time and time again. Make no mistake, with our Nebraska Nice, legislation and policy are NOT staying within a moderate middle. The pendulum has been swinging bit by bit to the extreme left for decades. Why does the influence on an elected official change from the year of campaigning, to less than a few months in the elected position? Furthermore, it creates problems with the voters to understand where a senator actually stands. As new party leadership, we want to break this philosophy. Our country and state needs strong leadership that is defined by solid values. We will seek votes on the record whenever possible, even if it leads to disappointment like Thursday. Republicans need to know where our elected officials stand when it matters most; especially on the issues of Life, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness. We can protect Nebraska from the far left with leaders that have convictions on values. Policy improvement over power is critical in 2023. Values over personalities. We are calling for the Pro-life issue to see the floor again in 2023. While LB626 may have limited options to find the floor, we know the pro-life issue has the ability to see the floor through an amendment on another piece of legislation. It is time to see if all our Senators are truly onboard with a pro-life bill (even if it is a different standard than 6 weeks) and can deliver a stronger standard than the current 20 weeks as a collective body. We know this takes grit, a no give up attitude, and extra work. As a disclaimer, we TRULY believe the overwhelming majority of our senators ARE PRO-LIFE. We believe it is critical to have a vote on pro-life occur this session even if it's not 6 weeks. We understand a different standard would likely have to be presented to pass. For the sake of the voting public, we need to see a vote on the record...even if that bill is a new standard. We know the standard will not likely appease the hurt or satisfy the hopes of the citizens/governing body who voted to affirm the Heartbeat bill standard of roughly 6 weeks. However, we need to chart a new course as a party and seek a vote on the record on a significant piece of legislation so our voters can make an informed decision in 2024 on the issue. | | CALL YOUR SENATOR AND INSIST ON A PRO LIFE AMENDMENT!!! | Finally, there is yet one more issue where we will point to...the need for new Rules. The NEGOP Legislative Plan adopted last August called to “Reduce the cloture vote requirement so that 17 Senators cannot block legislation.” Specifically, the leadership of the NEGOP suggests the level should match the number (3/5th's or 30) to override a Governor Veto. What is good to override the Governor is good to override a filibuster. This would preserve the filibuster capabilities, but bring the rule in line to move legislation forward that is strong enough to override the singular wishes of a Governor and the demands of a radical minority seeking to halt progress. We will bring this discussion forth again for the 2024 session. | | Lincoln Municipality Election - Tuesday, May 2nd | Have you called your mom in Lincoln to vote? Has your uncle in Lincoln returned his absentee ballot to the Election Commissioner? Have you called everyone you know in Lincoln to vote on May 2nd? We can’t emphasize voting enough. Lincoln is in dire need of leadership change. Law and order, taxes, and infrastructure proceeds in the wrong direction each and every day. Do everything in your power to make sure everyone you know in Lincoln with commonsense shows up to the polls on Tuesday or has returned their ballot. The extra legwork will MAKE A BIG DIFFERENCE!!! | | Voter ID | Voter ID language was unexpectedly delayed. We are now anticipating the presentation of these materials next week. The holdup is on the mail-in-voting language. There are four different options being debated by Republican leaders. Each option presents different challenges. From complying with the literal language of the amendment to being in observance of court rulings on the Constitutionality of a provision are the challenges being wrestled with. The quandary is that the language of the amendment doesn’t make it friendly to mail-in voting methods. Court cases on a national level for access to voting are being exhausted in legal research to insure a law passes constitutional muster. There is not a lot of middle ground between Constitutional cases and Nebraska’s adopted language. We will be quickly digesting the language after the release. Although Nebraska has traditionally taken a path of following the lead of other states, our Constitutional Amendment language is a new national standard for Voter ID. This is an opportunity for the state to show ingenuity and leadership on a critical issue the public has voted for by initiative (65.45% in favor). The language of the bill will demonstrate how tough of a path senators are willing to take with an observance of Constitutional cases that currently exist. Regardless of what is passed (strong or weak), it is likely to be challenged in court by the left desiring less election integrity. | | Video Footage Advances | We are pleased to report one of our planks to improve Government transparency, LB254, passed the second round. Senator Brewer’s bill to create a video archive of legislative debate was successful. We will continue to push for transparency to aid the public in evaluating their government. | Agriculture | It is time for a farm bill discussion again. The usual early talking points around the issue are occurring. SNAP programs, environmental provisions, and insurance coverages are standard operating procedures. However, the topic that stood out to us and really moves the needle is the protection amounts. These insurance amounts have not kept up with the cost of production. A report was issued that Nebraska is on pace to be over 5,400 nurses short. The funds are designed to be used to address the staffing shortage. | | Taxes and Spending | The budget committee voted out a budget that increased spending by 2.3% over the next two years. This was higher than the 1.5% the Governor was seeking. Medical reimbursement amounts and University adjustments are driving the increases. These additions in spending will raise Nebraska spending to just over $5.3 billion a year. Meanwhile, the economic forecasting board lowered revenue projections by $80 million to $6.36 billion. These numbers matter. The efforts to resolve a fiscal footnote of $900 million to continue to make tax relief work a reality continues. | A Disturbing Sign of the Times | Sometimes the state of affairs demonstrates the condition of the culture. Democrat Senator Megan Hunt had a complaint filed against her with the NADC. Senator Hunt received speeches of defense from both sides of the aisle defending her actions. Many Senators found the complaint absurd. Previously this year, Pastor Arin Hess also had a complaint of “Lobbying” filed against him with the NADC by a leftist organization. Pastor Hess calls himself a “Lobbyist for Jesus”. We noted that Senator Blood disagreed with the complaint dismissal of Pastor Hess. Furthermore, the PUBLIC statements of defense for a man who ministers to members of the Capitol were not there like they were for Senator Hunt. Both cases were being covered by the media. The difference of public treatment regarding two different public cases of NADC complaints signaled to us the sign of the times we live in. We will continue to fight for religious freedom and liberty at the NEGOP. We thank you for focusing on the spiritual needs of the Capitol, Pastor Hess. Free exercise on.... This is the last thing I wanted to hear since Sharla and I are busy finishing up prepping equipment and getting everything ready to start planting. So unless you're willing to get your hands dirty and spend 16 hours on a tractor with us, I need your help chipping in whatever you can.

[**Any amount you can contribute early in the campaign goes a long way in helping us grow our movement and reach these critical fundraising goals. name: Bob Casey
subject: Ever since I announced that I was running for re-election
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHAPIRO FOR PENNSYLVANIA
body:
National Republicans have been targeting me with negative ads to make it clear that flipping this seat is a top priority in their plan to retake Senate control in 2024.

Derek,

Ever since I announced that I was running for re-election, national Republicans have been targeting me with negative ads to make it clear that flipping this seat is a top priority in their plan to retake Senate control in 2024.

Well, I’m not done fighting for Pennsylvania’s hard-working families and I won’t back down to anyone — including Republicans in the Senate working to chip away at critical programs like Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid.

The stakes of this race are high, so we can’t afford to fall short now. That’s why I’m reaching out now before our end-of-month deadline.

**[Will you be one of the supporters to split a donation now between my campaign and Josh Shapiro to put us over the top of our critical $20,000 goal?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62542637/403764016/-1645383944?refcode=email_042023_jds-casey&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NTY5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZTI3MGU3Ni1kYmU2LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1A3YBJ5zD_EG-1ZELdMR9KD_USmexkoz8XXgRILrQHE=)**

No one knows better than I do that winning here in PA will be tough. That’s why we need to start this re-election campaign on the strongest footing possible.

Our deadline is nearly here. So, can you help us out, Derek?

**[Will you split $5 or any donation you can between my campaign and Josh Shapiro before tomorrow’s end-of-month deadline? I’m counting your support right now.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62542638/403764017/-1645383944?refcode=email_042023_jds-casey&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NTY5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZTI3MGU3Ni1kYmU2LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1A3YBJ5zD_EG-1ZELdMR9KD_USmexkoz8XXgRILrQHE=)** | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62542639/403764018/-1645383944?refcode=email_042023_jds-casey&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NTY5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZTI3MGU3Ni1kYmU2LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1A3YBJ5zD_EG-1ZELdMR9KD_USmexkoz8XXgRILrQHE=)

Our team is capable of great things if we work together. It’s going to take all of us to defend this seat and our Senate majority.

With gratitude,

Bob Casey

name: Kash Patel
subject: The most personal email I’ll ever send you
body:
[](http://email.email.a1warroom.com/c/eJxckcGOmzAQhp9muIFgDIYcOHRLaaWqPWzVSj2hCR6CN9gmxixKn74CJT30-nn0_f94yMyTHu41lC-AOGq1ACKID4B4-70Zv76Zr5_j2-01vsnvptgf8SMghvvM_yYfkoQN6WmnZROpmiQNrCKuM1lhlZZS5tFYi1NZYsVDn1VZkZV8RioKlamSRDlIWUW6xhRFmuMJUeSISSaqkxgoo2KgCoce8vQISijbyHvnTNI7E031GMK8HI1awHbhfvWcbNp6VvsEYKucpcDBKboDtldaxtgZEO0aTLe41fcMotk8oNyJYaVXA6J57HXAnsxM-mJBNEfLKh70ZQybDuPu644I7Wzn5qCN_sPdmSfN77zbqHs07mb2i7M0Heru7N2VPV24e-7-THM2sA0gGttP8a-34XT-9OXlxxVQdnbSCkTzHzxOKJrXe_OT3udvka_VvOlp0ossIU8vx8_1zvwNAAD___-spGI)

Hello Friend,

I never thought that my own government would spy on me.

I have always loved my country.

That's why I've dedicated my life to being a public defender, a federal prosecutor, and a legal liaison to the United States Armed Forces. I believe in the foundational values of America - life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness.

So when Devin Nunes asked me to help with the Russiagate investigation, I told him whatever we discovered about President Trump, _good or bad_ , it had to be published.

When I started my investigation, I hadn't even met President Trump. **But this investigation changed my life forever ...**

_Trump did NOT collude with Russia_.

**My investigation discovered the FBI 's unlawful investigation. And what I found makes me believe that they did it _intentionall_ y to get rid of President Trump.**

It's why I refer to the Russia Gate FBI cabal as government gangsters. They are crooks who abuse their power to extort our elected officials. Their deep connections with the media enable a manufacturing machine of fake news.

Once I found the truth, that's when the spying began...

Since then, I knew I had to do everything to help American patriots by offering financial assistance to whistleblowers, veterans, patriots, and some families of Jan 6 detainees.

As a former federal prosecutor, I know that we need protection if we are going to expose corrupt government gangsters.

clean_body: Hello Friend, I never thought that my own government would spy on me. I have always loved my country. That's why I've dedicated my life to being a public defender, a federal prosecutor, and a legal liaison to the United States Armed Forces. I believe in the foundational values of America - life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness. So when Devin Nunes asked me to help with the Russiagate investigation, I told him whatever we discovered about President Trump, good or bad , it had to be published. When I started my investigation, I hadn't even met President Trump. But this investigation changed my life forever ... Trump did NOT collude with Russia. My investigation discovered the FBI 's unlawful investigation. And what I found makes me believe that they did it intentionall y to get rid of President Trump. It's why I refer to the Russia Gate FBI cabal as government gangsters. They are crooks who abuse their power to extort our elected officials. Their deep connections with the media enable a manufacturing machine of fake news. Once I found the truth, that's when the spying began... Since then, I knew I had to do everything to help American patriots by offering financial assistance to whistleblowers, veterans, patriots, and some families of Jan 6 detainees. As a former federal prosecutor, I know that we need protection if we are going to expose corrupt government gangsters. I set a goal to raise $45,000 by the end of the week, and we're behind in reaching that goal. Conservatives, America-First patriots, or anyone who believes that Americans are innocent until proven guilty must join together in this fight. Some of the Jan 6 detainees have become victims of the abuses outlined above, and we cannot leave them in solitary confinement within the D.C. prison system. This two-tiered justice system is a complete sham and a mockery of our constitution. Due process has been destroyed. We cannot let the justice system be hijacked and used against us. Trump and the Jan 6 defendants are only the beginning. The radical left has weaponized the justice system against America-first patriots. Whether you're a whistleblower exposing deep state corruption, a parent protesting against trans ideology in our schools, or a pro-life activist praying outside of abortion clinics, the justice system can be used against you. We need an organization to defend our supporters, and that's what Kash Foundation is doing. Will you help me reach my goal by donating today? Please click any link below. Thank you, Kash Patel P.S. The radical left cannot destroy our reputations with the media like they used to. Now, they are assisted by the two-tier justice system. A legal foundation is needed to help America-first patriots with these struggles. I refuse to watch this sham of justice, but I need your help defending whistleblowers and America-first patriots. name: Tom Malinowski
subject: our mission before April ends…

body:
| | Hi there,

**Every day, our newsfeeds are inundated with stories about censorship in our public schools and attacks on teachers, librarians, and students.**

MAGA extremists claim they are protecting our children, while seeking to strip them of the basic freedoms essential to a good education – the freedom to speak, to ask hard questions, and to learn. But across America, people who don’t want to see their libraries defunded, or books about the Holocaust or America’s civil rights movement pulled from school shelves, are starting to organize.

[With November’s school elections coming, we must act now. We’re approaching the end of another month since Districts for Democracy launched, and we’re counting on your grassroots support. We’re taking it into our own hands to protect our kids and schools from the MAGA culture wars. Our mission is to encourage reasoned public debate on these issues, hold school board members and elected officials accountable, recruit and support candidates committed to democratic values and to opposing book bans and censorship.
We’re almost at the end of April, and there is no more important time than now to lay the critical groundwork for success.
